title: Hebbinghaus–Srivastav Open Problem
statement_text: Determine $\mathrm{disc}(\mathcal{A}_p)$ up to a constant factor for prime $p$, i.e., close the $O(\sqrt{\log p})$ multiplicative gap between the lower bound $c_1\sqrt{p}$ and the upper bound $c_2\sqrt{p\log p}$.
context_text: Hebbinghaus and Srivastav proved that for prime $p$ there exist positive constants $c_1, c_2$ such that $c_1\sqrt{p} \leq \mathrm{disc}(\mathcal{A}_p) \leq c_2\sqrt{p\log p}$, and wrote that closing the $O(\sqrt{\log p})$ multiplicative gap seemed to be a difficult open problem. Alon and Spencer (Theorem 13.1.1 in [1]) also contributed to the upper bound side.
paper_contribution: Removes the $\sqrt{\log p}$ factor from the upper bound, establishing $\mathrm{disc}(\mathcal{A}_p) = \Theta(\sqrt{p})$ for prime $p$ as a special case of the main Theorem 1.1.
notes: PDF source — math may be garbled; statement reconstructed from prose description of the gap between lower and upper bounds.
